commit.message:
feat(cli): implement programmatic Drizzle migration runner

Add runMigrations() utility to apps/cli that runs Drizzle ORM migrations
programmatically against the factory SQLite database. This eliminates the
need for manual pnpm db:migrate — migrations run automatically as part of
factory init and factory start.

Key features:
- Creates DB file and parent directories on first run
- Idempotent: no-op when already up to date
- Reports applied migration count via MigrationResult
- Custom MigrationError with dbPath/migrationsFolder context
- SQLite configured with WAL, busy_timeout, foreign_keys (matching control-plane)
- Always closes DB connection in finally block

Includes 10 unit tests covering first-run, idempotency, error cases,
WAL mode verification, and table creation validation.

Closes T141.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(control-plane): serve web-ui static files via @fastify/static (T120)

Add StaticServeModule and configureStaticServing() function for optional
single-origin deployment where API and web-ui SPA are served from the
same Fastify instance.

Implementation:
- @fastify/static registered with wildcard:false for reply.sendFile()
- Custom GET /* route serves actual files or falls back to index.html
- Fastify route priority ensures NestJS API routes always win
- Module activated by SERVE_STATIC=true + WEB_UI_DIST env vars
- configureStaticServing() exported for CLI entry point (T121)

Tests: 12 tests covering validation, static serving, SPA fallback,
API route precedence, and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): establish global data directory convention (T140)

Add paths module centralizing all path resolution for the factory's
global data directory (~/.copilot-factory/). Exports getFactoryHome(),
getDbPath(), getWorkspacesRoot(), getArtifactsRoot(), getMigrationsDir(),
and ensureFactoryHome() with FACTORY_HOME env var override support.

Includes 10 unit tests, vitest config, and test script for the CLI
workspace.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(infrastructure): implement deterministic markdown task parser

Add markdown task parser for the import pipeline (T113). Parses
directory of markdown task files into ImportManifest using existing
import schemas from T112.

Key features:
- discoverMarkdownTasks() main entry with FileSystem DI
- Metadata table parsing (| Field | Value | format)
- Section extraction (Description, Goal, Scope, etc.)
- Checkbox acceptance criteria extraction
- Dependency ref extraction from markdown links
- ExternalRef extraction from filenames (e.g., T045)
- Index.md parsing for task ordering hints
- Lenient parsing with ParseWarning collection
- Zod validation via ImportedTaskSchema

68 comprehensive tests covering all public functions and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(control-plane): add end-to-end dispatch integration test (T138)

Proves full task lifecycle BACKLOG → READY → ASSIGNED → dispatch →
IN_DEVELOPMENT → DEV_COMPLETE through the automation/dispatch pipeline
using FakeRunnerAdapter and FakeWorkspaceManager.

Verifies dispatch job completion, worker creation, heartbeat forwarding
(lease STARTING → RUNNING → COMPLETING), and task state transitions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(control-plane): wire WorkerDispatchService into AutomationService

Wire the full worker dispatch chain into AutomationService so each
automation cycle attempts to process pending WORKER_DISPATCH jobs.

- Add createWorkerSupervisorUnitOfWork and createHeartbeatUnitOfWork
  factory functions in application-adapters.ts
- Instantiate HeartbeatService, HeartbeatForwarderAdapter,
  InfrastructureAdapters, WorkerSupervisorService, and
  WorkerDispatchService in the AutomationService constructor
- Add processWorkerDispatches() fire-and-forget method to runCycle()
- Track active dispatch promises to prevent unbounded concurrency
- Add 10 new tests covering UoW factories and dispatch integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(infrastructure): wire workspace, runtime, and packet adapters into control-plane

Create adapter bridges in apps/control-plane/src/automation/ that wrap
WorkspaceManager, WorkspacePacketMounter, and CopilotCliAdapter from
@factory/infrastructure into WorkspaceProviderPort, PacketMounterPort,
and RuntimeAdapterPort application-layer port interfaces.

- createWorkspaceProviderAdapter: maps positional params to options objects
- createPacketMounterAdapter: delegates and discards MountPacketsResult
- createRuntimeAdapterBridge: bridges SupervisorRunContext/RunContext types
- createInfrastructureAdapters: top-level factory with env config support
- resolveInfrastructureConfig: reads WORKSPACES_ROOT with defaults
- 17 unit tests covering all adapter bridges and config resolution

Closes T136.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add createWorkerDispatchUnitOfWork() factory to application-adapters.ts,
bridging infrastructure database repositories to the WorkerDispatchUnitOfWork

spawn context (task packet, timeout settings, workspace paths, policy
snapshot) for tasks prior to worker creation.

Auto-load experiment config on --resume

When resuming and no --config was explicitly given, load the config.yaml
saved inside the experiment directory instead of production/config.yaml.
This prevents state_dict mismatches when resuming ablation runs that
used a different config (e.g., REPA disabled in run A).

commit.message:
Fix TL003 analyzer: add semantic verification for LINQ ToList

Same false-positive bug as TL011 - syntax-only matching would flag any
method named ToList(), not just System.Linq.Enumerable.ToList(). Rewrite
to use RegisterCompilationStartAction with SemanticModel verification.

Changes:
- Use RegisterCompilationStartAction to resolve System.Linq.Enumerable type
- Add IsLinqToListCall() to verify ToList() via SemanticModel.GetSymbolInfo
- Consolidate RegisterSyntaxNodeAction calls into a single registration
- Add negative tests for custom types with ToList() instance methods

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(application): implement WorkerDispatchService

Add WorkerDispatchService that claims WORKER_DISPATCH jobs from the queue,
resolves spawn context via WorkerDispatchContextPort, and delegates to
WorkerSupervisorService.spawnWorker(). Follows the existing job-processing
pattern (claim → process → complete/fail) but is async and non-self-
rescheduling since dispatch jobs are created on-demand by the scheduler.

New files:
- ports/worker-dispatch.ports.ts — port interfaces and context types
- services/worker-dispatch.service.ts — service factory and implementation
- services/worker-dispatch.service.test.ts — 11 unit tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix TL011 analyzer: add semantic verification for LINQ ToArray

Rewrite ToArrayLengthAnalyzer to use RegisterCompilationStartAction with
semantic model verification instead of syntax-only matching. This prevents
false positives on non-LINQ ToArray() calls (e.g., custom instance methods)
where the suggested .Any() fix would not compile.

Changes:
- Use RegisterCompilationStartAction to resolve System.Linq.Enumerable type
- Add IsLinqToArrayCall() to verify ToArray() via SemanticModel.GetSymbolInfo
- Consolidate RegisterSyntaxNodeAction calls into a single registration
- Add negative tests for custom types with ToArray() instance methods

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
update submodules (#312)

* 1st try at propagation, probably no changes

* how does it looks with new arm64 pool

* Add pipeline parameters for build pool names

Expose pool_name_windows_x64, pool_name_windows_arm64, and pool_name_linux
as pipeline-level parameters with current defaults. Pass them explicitly to
all c-build-tools templates instead of relying on template defaults.

This enables pool name overrides from the ADO UI and centralizes pool
configuration in a single file for easier rotation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* use latest c-build-tools master

* Use pool defaults from c-build-tools, keep UI override capability

Pool parameters now default to empty string. When empty, the
c-build-tools pipeline templates use their own built-in defaults.
When a value is provided via the ADO UI, it is passed through to
override the template default.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Show c-build-tools pool name defaults in pipeline UI

Replace empty pool name defaults with the actual values from
c-build-tools so they are visible in the ADO pipeline UI at build
time. Remove the now-unnecessary conditional parameter passing since
pool names always have values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Build complete asynchronous audio processing API

- FastAPI app with /analyze, /status/{task_id}, /tracklist/{tracklist_id} endpoints
- Celery task pipeline: download → segment → fingerprint → aggregate
- yt-dlp download task with SoundCloud HLS AAC support and ramdisk output
- Essentia SBic transition detection with BeatTracker/onset fallback
- ffmpeg snippet extraction (12s, 16kHz mono WAV)
- Shazamio fingerprinting with asyncio.run wrapper, rate-limited 15/m
- SQLAlchemy models (Tracklist, Track) with UUID PKs
- Alembic initial migration for tracklists and tracks tables
- Docker Compose with 7 services, health checks, 4GB tmpfs ramdisk
- 21 passing tests covering all endpoints and task behaviors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

SKA Op2 partial eval: inline/memo/loop/fresh-scan for known formulas

When scan_cell encounters Nock 2 and the formula expression (c) has a
fully-known cape, the resolved formula is now analyzed at compile time
instead of falling back to NOMM_I2 (runtime eval). Four sub-cases:

- Inline: [0 ax] or [1 val] → rewrite as NOMM_7 (compose)
- Memo hit: same (formula, compatible-subject) → reuse DS2 body
- Loop: formula on fols_stack with subsumption → DS2 backedge
- Fresh scan: push frame, recurse, pop → DS2 with analyzed body

Also fixes run_nomm1 NOMM_2 handler for ax==0 (Op2-originated DS2):
uses nock_ex(core, bell.fol) instead of nock_op9_continue which would
crash on axis 0.

8 new tests added; 362/362 passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(backlog): add worker dispatch tasks T132–T139

Add 8 new tasks to E009 (Worker Runtime & Execution) epic to close the
gap between task scheduling and worker execution. The scheduler creates
WORKER_DISPATCH jobs but no service consumes them, leaving tasks stuck
in ASSIGNED state.

New tasks:
- T132: WorkerDispatchService in application layer
- T133: Unit tests for dispatch service
- T134: Wire dispatch unit-of-work adapter
- T135: HeartbeatForwarderPort adapter
- T136: Wire workspace/runtime/packet infrastructure adapters
- T137: Integrate dispatch into AutomationService
- T138: End-to-end dispatch integration test
- T139: Update worker-runner package exports

Also updates E009 epic task table with new tasks and corrects
T043–T045 status to done.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update Code Security SCA Page (#35113)

* Update Code Security SCA Page

This PR updates the Software Composition Analysis (SCA) documentation to make the product model and UI views easier to understand.

Changes:
1. Adds a How it works section that explains Static SCA (repo scans) vs Runtime SCA (APM) and how advisory ingestion impacts results.
2. Reorganizes content into Key capabilities with clearer user workflows (prioritize vulnerabilities, view by repository, PR Gates, library inventory, APM context).
3. Adds Understanding SCA views to explain the difference between Repositories Explorer (commit-scoped snapshot) and Vulnerabilities Explorer (continuously matched), including an example.
4. Documents retroactive advisory matching so customers understand why new CVEs can appear without rerunning scans.
5. Keeps existing setup and language support references and updates “Next steps” accordingly.

* Update content/en/security/code_security/software_composition_analysis/_index.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 110 hardening tests: Nock reference vectors, crash recovery, atoms

Test suite: 245 → 355 tests (all passing)

New test categories:
- 63 Nock reference tests from norm/tests.json: all 12 opcodes + autocons,
  builds subject+formula as nouns, runs NOCK, compares result with =NOUN.
  Includes 4 crash tests (axis 0, slot in atom, inc cell, bad condition).
- 20 crash recovery tests: nock atom, opcode not direct, op2/4/5/6/7/8/9/
  10/11 structural crashes, slot axis 0, slot in atom, op12 unimplemented,
  %tame atom clue. Each verifies REPL recovers after longjmp.
- 20 Forth primitive smoke tests: DUP/DROP/SWAP/OVER/ROT, arithmetic,
  comparison, bitwise (AND/OR/XOR/INV/NEG).
- 10 indirect atom hardening: Nock 3/4/5 on indirect atoms, CONS with
  indirect head/tail, JAM/CUE roundtrip, BNSUB to direct boundary,
  BN+ double max_direct.

Also:
- tools/gen_norm_tests.py: generates T/BEFORE lines from tests.json
- Increased QEMU timeout to 60s for larger suite

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add direction badges to distinguish request/response/client messages

Each message card now shows its communication direction:
- 📤 REQUEST — system, user, tool results (sent to OpenAI)
- 📥 RESPONSE — assistant, tool_calls, structured (received from OpenAI)
- 🖥️ CLIENT — handoff context, tool execution (runs locally)
- 🖥️ → 📤 — tool results (executed locally, then sent to API)

Payload toggles are color-coded per direction (blue/green/orange)
with matching border colors on the expanded payload blocks.
Dark mode (slate theme) adjustments included.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: editable agent prompts + <answer> tag extraction + bypass copilot refusal

- Add DEFAULT_PRO_ROLE / DEFAULT_CON_ROLE with <answer> tag instruction and
  'AI辩论模拟测试项目' framing to bypass copilot's engineering-only restriction
- extractAIResponse(): try <answer>...</answer> tag extraction first, fall back
  to line-filter; much more reliable than heuristic filtering
- runDebate(): accept optional proRoleCtx/conRoleCtx params, override defaults
- web.ts: pass msg.proRoleCtx / msg.conRoleCtx into runDebate()
- useVyiEngine: startTerminalDebate() accepts proRoleCtx/conRoleCtx, sends them
- TerminalDebateView: collapsible settings bar above each terminal panel with
  editable <textarea> for role prompt (locked while debate is running)
- App.tsx: manage proRoleCtx/conRoleCtx state, pass to view + startTerminalDebate
- CSS: .terminal-panel-wrapper, .agent-settings-bar, .agent-settings-panel,
  .agent-prompt-textarea, .settings-hint, .settings-locked styles

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
MEMO_006: FastAPI TTS servers, audio interrupt, routing fix

- Nginx path mismatch fixed (CT102 accepts both path formats)
- All three TTS servers rewritten as FastAPI with uvicorn
- Piper fallback routes through smart router with forced engine
- Added stopAudio() interrupt for user-takes-priority behavior
- OmniChat interrupts Hugh voice on record or text send

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: H.U.G.H. — Hyper Unified Guardian & Harbor-master | Stigmergic Workshop UI

commit.message:
Add interactive message flow visualizations to all exercises

Adds 13 interactive step-through widgets across 9 exercise doc files:
- 00_setup: 1 widget (isolated, 3 steps)
- 01_llm_basics: 3 widgets (growing, independent, structured)
- 02_tool_use: 2 widgets (single function call, multi-iteration loop)
- 03_single_agent: 1 widget (growing, 9-step multi-turn)
- 04_sequential: 1 widget (structured 3-stage pipeline)
- 05_concurrent: 1 widget (isolated fan-out + aggregation)
- 06_group_chat: 2 widgets (brainstorm + maker-checker)
- 07_handoff: 1 widget (structured triage + specialist)
- 08_magentic: 1 widget (task-ledger manager-worker, 7 steps)

Widget content informed by actual exercise runs against Azure OpenAI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: align web-ui PaginatedResponse type with API response format

The API returns { data: T[], meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages } }
but the web-ui expected { items: T[], page, limit, total, hasMore }.

Updated PaginatedResponse type, all component field accesses (.items →
.data, .total → .meta.total), and all test mocks to match.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
